what_it_does: It is the folder-local charter and automatic memory for agents entering the design corpus: it defines what the folder is, the read-first boot order, the file map, permitted operations, register authority, keeper role, and behavioral laws, and it mandates self-maintenance of the folder's own documentation.
observations: Declares itself automatic memory + behaviour for agents spawned in the design corpus.
Describes the folder as a front-end design corpus and living model: registry-as-truth, tokenised, addressed, self-describing.
Specifies a read-first boot order: capstone, register.json, Feature & Function Inventory, conventions.md.

Maps files and wiring: tokens.json -> emit.py -> design-system.css; addresses.json -> parse.py -> element-map.json; register.json -> gallery.py -> index.html; plus check.py, refcheck.py, symbols.py, codeedges.py, mechanisms.json.

Lists operations: change look from one place, address+map, find missing, refresh gallery, generate grounded mockups, catch traceability drift, index code symbols, map code dependencies, register mechanisms, local-swarm moves, extend-by-registration.
States register authority D-1: views[].status overrides produced.wave1[].status; produced must mirror views[].status; status ladder is planned -> drafting -> drafted -> quality-passed -> approved.
Defines the keeper role: grow the substrate, keep it true/coherent/self-describing, update charter and conventions in the same pass as system changes.
Behavioral laws: no fiction, edit data not generated files, extend-by-registration, no priority/ranking language, no versioning, expansive substrate, prove by use.
With Tim: recognition-based judgement, render outcomes, offer options not binary, be the brain not an echo.
Never list: no fictional features, no hand-editing generated files, no ranking, no v2 copies, no modifying ~/company, no drift, no trimming substrate.
standouts: Self-describing substrate governed by a charter that recursively requires updating itself.
Heavy machinery pipeline turning JSON registries into CSS, element maps, and gallery.
Register authority rule reconciling produced status down to view status.
Keeper's first duty to keep charter and conventions true whenever systems change.
conventions: Charter/keeper pattern for folder-local agent memory.
Extensive cross-references to sibling files and scripts.
Universal coordinate schemes ui:// and code://.
Generated files produced by scripts from JSON registries, never hand-edited.
Status ladder and authority rule for register consistency.
No priority/ranking language and no dated copies.
Self-maintenance clause requiring docs updated in the same change pass.
Markdown with headings, blockquotes, lists, and horizontal rules.
concerns: The file is long and dense; a fresh agent could miss details despite the boot order.
Self-maintenance requirement creates circular-drift risk if the charter lags real changes.
Heavy reliance on external scripts and registries whose schemas are not shown here.
Project-specific shorthand codes (X10, X11, X14, X17, G-9, D-1) are used without definition.
References Tim's global working-style as external memory not present in this file.
notes: A recursive, self-updating charter that turns the design folder into an addressed, tokenised, machine-readable substrate with strict no-fiction and no-ranking rules.
questions: What is the exact schema of register.json and the token/address registries?
Which read-first files are guaranteed to exist and in what state?
What do the shorthand codes X10, X11, X14, X17, G-9, and D-1 denote?
How is Tim's global working-style loaded and what does it contain?
What is the precise boundary between this design corpus and the real app under ~/company?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the automatic memory and behaviour charter for the design folder; the file does exactly that by enumerating boot order, file map, operations, authority rules, and keeper laws, so purpose and actual content align.
apparent_themes: Grounded design: no fiction, traceable to real app features.
intention_signals: To make the design corpus bootable and governable by an autonomous agent: give it a single source of behavioural truth, enforce structural integrity through scripts and registries, and keep the model aligned with the real application without drifting into fictional or ranked claims.
novelty: A folder-local agent charter that recursively mandates updating itself and the conventions file, combined with a universal coordinate system (ui:// / code://) and a generated gallery/maps pipeline, treating design as an addressable substrate rather than static documentation.
contribution: Provides the behavioural contract and boot instructions for the design corpus, including the read-first chain, file map, keeper duties, and no-fiction/no-ranking laws, which a fused system would need to preserve to keep the substrate self-describing and agent-governed.
summary_for_embedding: CLAUDE.md is the self-updating keeper's charter for the company's design corpus, specifying folder purpose, read-first boot order, file map, registry-driven generation pipeline, register authority, keeper role, and behavioral laws.
intention_for_embedding: To give any agent entering the design folder an automatic memory of what the corpus is, how its files and scripts relate, and how to grow it without fiction, ranking, or drift.
